skill.name: implementing-with-tdd
skill.description: Guides agents through test-first implementation using project test, lint, and task completion commands.
skill.summary: Apply test-driven development for project code changes with clear red-green-refactor steps.
skill.author: BPSAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution patterns and one weak cryptography blocker, but review found only Markdown examples for testing, linting, and task status commands. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential access, network exfiltration, prompt injection, or cryptographic code in SKILL.md. The remaining concern is operational: the skill instructs agents to run local commands that can modify repository files or task state.

low_findings.title: Local Command Guidance in Documentation
low_findings.description: The static Ruby/shell backtick findings are true positives for command-shaped Markdown examples, not hidden executable code. The commands run local tests, lint checks, coverage, and project task updates, with no evidence of exfiltration or obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains visible Markdown command examples for expected developer workflows. The commands are project-local and do not include suspicious network, credential, or obfuscation behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The high static blocker at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive. That line is the skill description and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption call, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter description text only. There is no cryptographic API or weak algorithm evidence at the flagged location.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes pytest, ruff, bpsai-pair, and task completion commands as instructions. These are legitimate for a TDD workflow but can affect local files or task state when executed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command examples are directly present in SKILL.md and match the static external command findings. Their purpose is transparent developer automation, so severity is low.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose test coverage when fixes and features move quickly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a test-first workflow with project checks and completion steps.
actual_capabilities: Directs agents to start task work before changing code.
Defines a red-green-refactor workflow for bug fixes and features.
Lists project pytest commands for targeted, full, coverage, and failed-test runs.
Documents project test naming and fixture conventions.
Includes lint and combined CI commands for final verification.
Connects passing checks to the project task completion workflow.
limitations: It is tailored to a Python project that uses pytest, ruff, and bpsai-pair commands.
It does not generate tests or implementation code by itself.
It assumes the repository has the referenced test paths and task lifecycle skill.
Some commands can modify files or task status when the agent runs them.

use_cases.title: Fix a Regression with Coverage
use_cases.description: Use the skill to write a failing pytest case before changing production code.

use_cases.title: Add a Feature Safely
use_cases.description: Use the red-green-refactor sequence to keep feature work tied to executable tests.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Contributions
use_cases.description: Use consistent test commands, lint checks, and task updates during AI-assisted coding.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Small Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a focused bug fix.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-with-tdd skill to fix this bug. Start by writing a failing pytest test, then implement the smallest change needed.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Tested Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a new behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-with-tdd skill for this feature. Create tests for the expected behavior, run them red, implement the feature, and rerun checks.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor with Safety Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-with-tdd skill to refactor this module. Preserve behavior with tests first, keep tests green, then run linting and CI checks.

prompt_templates.title: Complete a Task End to End
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for project workflow integration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the implementing-with-tdd skill for this task from start to finish. Update task status, follow red-green-refactor, run all checks, and prepare completion notes.

output_examples.input: Implement a bug fix using TDD.
output: A failing test is added for the reported behavior.
The implementation is changed only enough to make the test pass.
Targeted tests and lint checks are run before task completion.

output_examples.input: Add a new feature with project checks.
output: The task is marked in progress before coding begins.
Feature tests document the expected behavior.
The agent runs pytest, ruff, and the combined project check command.

output_examples.input: Refactor a module without changing behavior.
output: Existing behavior is protected with tests before edits.
Refactoring proceeds while keeping the test suite green.
Completion notes include the checks that passed.

best_practices: Start with the smallest failing test that proves the requested behavior.
Run targeted tests before broad checks to keep feedback fast.
Record the verification commands and results before completing the task.
anti_patterns: Do not implement production code before creating a failing test when TDD is appropriate.
Do not skip lint or CI checks after making code changes.
Do not update task status to done before tests and checks pass.

faq.question: What type of project is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for Python projects that use pytest, ruff, and the bpsai-pair task workflow.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands that an agent may run as part of the requested development workflow.

faq.question: Can it be used for bug fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow starts by writing a failing test that reproduces the bug.

faq.question: Can it be used for feature work?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports feature implementation when expected behavior can be captured in tests.

faq.question: What checks does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends targeted pytest runs, full tests, coverage checks, ruff linting, and the project CI command.

faq.question: What security review result applies?
faq.answer: The audit found low risk. Command examples are visible and project-local, but users should review commands before execution.
